card.intuition: Dynamic variables defined in CSS that can be reused throughout a stylesheet and changed at runtime using JavaScript or media queries.
card.analogy: A global 'find and replace' that stays alive: instead of writing 'hex code #3b82f6' 50 times, you define `--brand-blue: #3b82f6`. If you want to change to 'brand-red', you only change it in one place, and the whole site updates instantly.
when_to_use: Implementing Dark Mode / Light Mode themes
Storing consistent design tokens (colors, spacing, font sizes)
Dynamic components where styles change based on user input (via JS)
gotchas: CSS variables follow the cascade and inheritance rules! If you define `--color` on a parent, all children inherit it unless they override it
You access them using the `var()` function, like `color: var(--brand-blue);`

exercise.prompt: Complete the CSS variable definition and usage.
hints: Use the 'var' function and the variable name starting with '--'
exercise.test_code:
assert "var" in solution
assert "--" in solution
print("PASS")
